FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Fintech and Big Tech push into banking charters while regional M&A ticks up and Fed discord simmers over rate cuts.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter to offer deposits and lending directly under FDIC regulation, escalating fintech's banking ambitions.
Sony gets OCC conditional approval for trust charter; Connectia Trust stablecoin platform launching 2027.
Regional consolidation: Hometown Bank (MA) acquiring NH peer for $160M; Bank7 pursuing 71% Century Bank stake after forced sale triggered by shareholder loan default.
Fed minutes expected to reveal rate-cut divide between hawks and doves, with internal squabbling likely to extend across multiple meetings.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Aggregation sector hits turbulence as Morningstar-Pello deal fails; meanwhile, RIA consolidation accelerates with big advisor moves and regional acquisitions.

bullets.text: Morningstar's ByAllAccounts sale to Pello collapsing—another blow to fragile account aggregation space as Plaid gears up for IPO and CFPB deliberates permanent data-fee rules.
Wavvest snags $1.4B Vinnie Maliakkal from Charles Schwab as managing director, signaling continued poaching of top talent to AI-native platforms.
Choreo acquires $700M planning-focused RIA under new CEO Jason Van de Loo, continuing aggressive consolidation streak.
Fisher Investments hit $450B AUM (up from $112B in 2019) through pure organic growth and media blitz—remaining M&A-resistant while competitors consolidate.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: M&A momentum continues with Amwins/Dragoneer reaffirming Steadfast bid and Zurich clearing Beazley; regulatory headwinds emerge on autonomous vehicles and cargo maintenance.

bullets.text: Amwins and Dragoneer extend exclusivity four weeks on their A$7.7B ($5.34B) Steadfast bid as deal marches toward shareholder vote.
European Commission green-lights Zurich's Beazley acquisition under simplified review with no competition concerns cited.
NHTSA chief signals mounting regulatory pressure on AV companies after pattern of autonomous vehicles interfering with emergency responders.
UPS maintenance gaps on Boeing engine parts tied to Louisville crash could trigger new FAA-mandated protocols across cargo operators.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Equifax's $750M Mexico play leads the day; credit fundraising hits record highs while capital rotation accelerates toward AI data centers.

bullets.text: Equifax acquiring Círculo de Crédito for $750M to establish foothold in Mexico's fast-growing credit bureau market.
Credit fundraising posted record H1 2026 levels with momentum potentially carrying into full-year record—defying headline skepticism on credit markets.
Data center assets reshaping CMBS composition as AI infrastructure buildout drives capital rotation away from traditional CRE into spec facilities.
Midwest multifamily 'safe-haven' narrative masks credit and structural vulnerabilities—Trepp flagging operational risks beneath headline consensus.

PE & Deals.lede: Five stories tracking institutional capital redeployment—from Russell Investments' consortium acquisition to record PE fundraising and secondaries repositioning.

bullets.text: B Capital leading CalPERS-backed consortium to acquire Russell Investments, a major institutional asset-allocation platform.
PE fundraising hit record highs in Q2 2026 with GP-led secondaries expected to triple; defense sector emerging as collaboration-dependent return driver.
Temasek pivoting away from underperforming EMEA directs toward AI, private credit, and core-plus infrastructure by 2031; Australia's Rest expanding PE/infrastructure allocations under new leadership.
Patria closed $677m secondaries fund targeting smaller deals to differentiate amid persistent DPI headwinds in the market.

Markets & Macro.lede: European banks dodge capital rules via synthetics; dollar's bond-market shield eroding; PE firms racing to exit Hologic LBO.

bullets.text: European banks weaponizing synthetic risk transfers to shed loan risk without shrinking portfolios as ECB stress tests tighten—regulatory arbitrage accelerating.
Foreign central banks dumping dollar reserves amid U.S. geopolitical friction; the historical bond-market bid underpinning dollar strength is deteriorating.
TPG/Blackstone hunting $4bn+ exit on 2024 Hologic acquisition ($6.2bn entry) to deleverage heavily debt-financed LBO—capital return pressure building.

Newsletters.lede: Banks plot payment network M&A to dodge fee caps; wealth managers pivot to long-short strategies; SEC floats accredited investor expansion; and State Street wins Trump admin treasury business.

bullets.text: Wall Street banks exploring acquisition of card payment networks to circumvent interchange fee caps that have cost issuers tens of billions in revenue.
Direct-indexing SMAs losing tax-loss harvesting edge in rising markets; wealth managers pivoting to long-short strategies to maintain tax-alpha narrative and justify fees.
SEC proposal would grant accredited investor status to clients under registered advisor advice, bypassing net-worth/income gates—advisor community split on access expansion vs. compliance risk.
State Street's SPYM ETF selected as core holding for Trump administration government-sponsored accounts alongside Vanguard and BlackRock products.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Data quality emerges as AI's real moat; meanwhile, structural questions about AI governance and economic policy frame longer-term industry risks.

bullets.text: Data, not compute, is AI's decisive edge: Meta, xAI, and Palantir are winning on access to high-quality, verifiable datasets rather than model size alone—a shift that reorders competitive positioning.

AI governance legitimacy gap: Constitutional frameworks like Claude's raise real consent and rule-of-law questions that financial institutions need to grapple with as AI becomes operationally critical infrastructure.
Agentic workflows are replacing prompt engineering: Anthropic's 12-recipe playbook signals a maturation layer where AI moves from chatbot interface to autonomous task delegation.

all.lede: Five stories spanning fintech regulation, asset management M&A, and payments infrastructure—Klarna eyes banking charter while banks explore fee-cap workarounds.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for FDIC ILC charter to directly offer deposits and lending under U.S. regulation, marking major regulatory milestone for Swedish fintech.
Amwins/Dragoneer reaffirm A$7.7B ($5.3B) bid for Steadfast Group with extended exclusivity; Australian consolidation play moving toward shareholder vote.
B Capital-led consortium including CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments, a major institutional asset-allocation platform with broad index and retail exposure.
Wall Street banks exploring payment network acquisitions to sidestep interchange fee caps that have cost issuers tens of billions—structural workaround emerging.
